---
title: "MoECOT Runtime and Multi-Core Orchestration"
chapter_id: "moecot-runtime-and-multi-core-orchestration"
part_id: "routing-compression-evidence-implementation"
status: "conceptual"
last_updated: "2026-06-24"
primary_sources:
  - "octopus_router"
  - "rmi"
  - "benchmaxxing"
  - "beastbrain"
  - "viea"
  - "scf"
  - "talos"
  - "moecot_md"
  - "moecot"
evidence_level: "argument"
claim_label: "Design rationale"
open_evidence_gaps:
  - "Assigned source texts have not yet been ingested or summarized."
  - "No Codex tests have been implemented or run."
  - "Chapter prose is a scaffold, not a completed manuscript draft."
---

# MoECOT Runtime and Multi-Core Orchestration

## Chapter status

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Chapter ID | `moecot-runtime-and-multi-core-orchestration` |
| Part | Part III - Routing, Compression, Evidence, and Implementation |
| Status | conceptual |
| Last updated | 2026-06-24 |
| Primary source records | `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `benchmaxxing`, `beastbrain`, `viea`, `scf`, `talos`, `moecot_md`, `moecot` |
| Claim label | Design rationale |
| Evidence level | argument |
| Source ingestion state | Source records assigned; source texts not yet ingested. |
| Test state | Planned only; no tests have been run. |

## Drafting guardrail

This stub is derived from `book_structure.json` and the source inventory only. It does not claim that the listed source documents have been ingested, summarized, or independently verified.

## Problem

The book needs a concrete implementation reference for governed multi-core orchestration, ledgers, replay, and benchmark promotion.

## Why existing approaches are insufficient

A purely abstract stack can fail to specify runtime state, registry boundaries, readiness gates, replay, and operational artifacts.

## Core Claim

[moecot-runtime-and-multi-core-orchestration.core, label: Design rationale, support: argument] MoECOT should be treated as the implementation-reference layer for a governed low-parameter multi-core ASI stack once its authenticated source is fully mined.

## Mechanism

- Use MoECOT as a runtime crosswalk for router, specialist, ledger, benchmark, and replay concepts.
- Keep direct MoECOT claims conservative until the primary source is ingested.
- Map MoECOT mechanisms to other stack layers rather than making it a separate silo.

## Interfaces

- Routing selects cores.
- SCFs govern replacement.
- Evidence and replay ledgers evaluate runtime changes.

## Invariants

- MoECOT claims stay at argument level until source notes exist.
- Implementation evidence is separated from design argument.
- Runtime promotion follows readiness gates.

## Failure modes

- Overclaiming unavailable source content.
- Treating implementation branding as architecture proof.
- Losing cross-layer traceability.

## Minimal implementation

A MoECOT crosswalk chapter with authenticated-source gap markers and runtime-interface tables.

## Codex test plan

| Test | Purpose | Status |
|---|---|---|
| MoECOT source-ingestion gate | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Runtime crosswalk completeness test | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |
| Readiness/replay mapping review | Support or falsify this chapter's layer claim. | planned; not run |

## Source crosswalk

| Source ID | Title | Planned use |
|---|---|---|
| `octopus_router` | Octopus Router Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Lightweight head/router with dynamically loaded specialist arms and local boundaries. |
| `rmi` | Ratcheting Modular Intelligence | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmark pressure, residual escrow, verified modular capability, regression preservation. |
| `benchmaxxing` | Benchmaxxing: The Performance Ratchet | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Benchmarks as pressure surfaces, saturation -> regression, harder frontier, anti-Goodhart safeguards. |
| `beastbrain` | BeastBrain Cognitive Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Architecture lineage. Organism paradigm, SSD-native/geometrically verified intelligence. |
| `viea` | Verified Intent-to-Execution Architecture | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Keystone source. Human intent -> command contracts -> artifacts -> routing -> runtime targets -> verification -> deployment -> feedback. |
| `scf` | Stable Capability Fields | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Use public release v1.0 when available. Stable boundaries, replacement, bounded authority, recoverable evolution. |
| `talos` | Talos Protocol | Planned use from inventory/manifest: AI labor OS. Deterministic cognitive manufacturing, typed jobs, control planes, auditability, tool isolation. |
| `moecot_md` | moecot_agent_whitepaper.md | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Markdown export/source version. |
| `moecot` | MoECOT-Agent Architecture Whitepaper | Planned use from inventory/manifest: Concrete implementation evidence: governed low-parameter multi-core runtime, readiness gates, ledgers, replay. |

## Summary

This chapter currently establishes the structural slot for this layer of the ASI stack. The next drafting pass should ingest assigned sources, separate source-derived claims from design hypotheses, and update Appendix C when support states change.
